meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no coding experience (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no coding experience. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Founders: Analytics Setup Guide (No-Code AI Approach)
content.seo_description: Stop flying blind. Learn how to implement professional analytics without coding experience using Antigravity and Claude Code for your SaaS MVP.
content.answer_block: You don't need a degree in data science to track your SaaS growth. By leveraging AI-driven agents like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can inject event-tracking scripts into your codebase instantly. This setup allows you to monitor user behavior, validate product-market fit, and iterate based on real revenue-driving data.
content.intro:
Every SaaS founder reaches the same inflection point: you have a product, but you’re operating in a vacuum. You’re guessing what users want, hoping they click the 'Subscribe' button, and praying they don't churn. Without data, you aren't building a business; you’re building a hobby.

The barrier to entry has always been the technical complexity of implementing event tracking. But in the era of AI engineering, 'no coding experience' is no longer a valid excuse for poor visibility. We are going to bridge the gap between intuition and insight, using automated workflows to wire your product for deep analytics without writing a single line of boilerplate code.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before touching code, identify the three events that signal actual revenue growth (e.g., Sign-up, Add to Cart, Subscription).
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current product flow and suggest the 3 most important user events to track for a B2B SaaS MVP to maximize conversion insights.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project files and list the key user interaction points that should be tracked for conversion analytics.'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Provider
steps.description: Select a lightweight provider like PostHog or Mixpanel and generate the necessary API keys.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an integration module for PostHog analytics using their official SDK, ensuring environment variables are handled securely.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Install posthog-js and create a wrapper utility file to initialize the client using process.env.POSTHOG_KEY.'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Flows
steps.description: Inject tracking events into your critical UI components to capture user intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify the 'Sign Up' and 'Purchase' buttons in my codebase and inject the corresponding tracking events using the initialized analytics client.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Find the sign-up component and add a PostHog capture event on the successful submission handler.'

steps.title: Implement Session Replay
steps.description: Visual data is better than quantitative data. Enable session replay to watch how users interact with your interface.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the session recording feature for my analytics provider to help me debug user friction points in the checkout funnel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Enable session recording in the analytics initialization file and verify it is only active in production.'

steps.title: Validate and Deploy
steps.description: Ensure your events are firing correctly before pushing to your production environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script that simulates a user journey and verifies that the analytics events are being sent to the server.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run the test suite and confirm that the analytics events are captured in the mock environment.'

pro_tips: Always debounce your event calls to avoid hitting API rate limits during high-traffic surges.
Tag your users with distinct IDs immediately upon login to ensure cross-device tracking accuracy.
Exclude your own developer IP addresses from the analytics dashboard to prevent data pollution.
Focus on 'Retention' events over 'Vanity' events; track what keeps them, not just what brings them.
Use feature flags alongside analytics to A/B test UI changes without redeploying your entire application.
content.next_steps: Once your events are flowing, build a dashboard in your analytics provider that shows your 'Conversion Funnel' from landing page to paid subscription.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark. Implement these tracking measures today to turn your MVP into a data-backed revenue engine. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
